description: Add E2E test coverage for the Chrome desktop notification that fires when a browser tool requires confirmation approval. The notification feature was shipped but its E2E test story was never completed.

userStories.title: Add E2E test verifying confirmation notification appears and clears on approval
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want an E2E test that verifies a Chrome desktop notification is created when a browser tool triggers a confirmation dialog, and is cleared when the user responds, so that the notification lifecycle is covered by automated tests.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E test starts MCP server without skipPermissions on a non-trusted domain (127.0.0.2)
E2E test calls an interact-tier tool (e.g. browser_close_tab) that triggers confirmation
E2E test verifies chrome.notifications.create was called with title 'OpenTabs — Approval Required'
E2E test approves the confirmation via the side panel and verifies the notification is cleared
E2E test verifies no notification is shown when the side panel is already open before the tool call
userStories.notes:
This test belongs in e2e/permissions.e2e.ts alongside the existing confirmation dialog tests. The existing test infrastructure already has:

- Custom fixture with MCP server started with `OPENTABS_SKIP_PERMISSIONS: ''` to disable bypass
- Uses 127.0.0.2 as non-trusted domain so interact-tier tools trigger confirmation
- Helper functions: `waitForConfirmationDialog`, `clickAllowOnce`, `openSidePanel`
- `callToolWithProgress` for concurrent tool call + side panel interaction

The notification feature is in `platform/browser-extension/src/confirmation-badge.ts`:
- `notifyConfirmationRequest()` creates a Chrome notification with `chrome.notifications.create`
- Uses a single consolidated notification ID: `'opentabs-confirmation'`
- Notification title is `'OpenTabs — Approval Required'`
- Notification is suppressed when `isSidePanelOpen()` returns true (from `side-panel-toggle.ts`)
- `clearConfirmationBadge(id)` decrements and clears notification when count reaches 0

**Testing Chrome notifications in Playwright:** Chrome notifications are system-level and NOT directly observable via Playwright page APIs. The recommended approach is to test the notification lifecycle indirectly by:
1. Verifying the confirmation dialog appears in the side panel (already tested)
2. Intercepting the background service worker's calls to `chrome.notifications.create` by reading extension storage or logs
3. Using `extension_get_state` or `extension_get_logs` browser tools to verify notification state

Alternatively, check if the extension's internal log (exposed via `extension_get_logs`) records notification creation/clearing. The confirmation-badge module logs nothing by default, but the unit tests in `confirmation-badge.test.ts` mock `chrome.notifications` — the E2E test can verify the same behavior by checking that the confirmation flow works end-to-end (tool blocks -> side panel shows dialog -> user approves -> tool succeeds) and that badge text is set/cleared (observable via `chrome.action.getBadgeText`).

**Simplest viable approach:** Since `chrome.notifications.create` is not directly observable from Playwright, focus the E2E test on verifiable side effects:
1. Badge text changes (set to count, cleared to '') — use `extension_get_state` to check
2. Side panel suppression — when panel is already open, badge count still increments but notification is suppressed (this is already unit-tested, E2E can verify the flow works without hanging)

Existing confirmation E2E tests in permissions.e2e.ts (around line 131) already test the full confirmation flow. The new test should add assertions about badge state and the notification-suppression-when-panel-is-open behavior.
